description: Set parameters for a mechanical analysis. The mechanical process is on by default. This command controls mechanical calculation activity, timestep settings, and slave/master coupling for multi-physics simulations.
notes: Mechanical process is ON by default
timestep automatic: default mode - calculates stable timestep from stiffnesses and masses
timestep fix: fixed timestep - skips calculations for performance, may scale masses in FLAC3D
timestep increment: controls maximum timestep increase rate (default 1.05), can decrease freely
timestep maximum: sets upper bound on timestep (default: unbounded)
slave mode: mechanical analysis runs to equilibrium after each master step
substep: max steps for slave mode
time-total: can be reset to any value, accumulates during cycling
FLAC3D static analysis: timestep is arbitrary, default 1.0, masses scaled for stability
See documentation: 'Mechanical Timestep Determination for Numerical Stability' for mass scaling details

python_sdk_alternative.python_code:
# Get timestep
dt = itasca.timestep()

# Set fixed timestep
itasca.set_timestep(1e-6)

# Get accumulated mechanical time
time = itasca.mech_age()

# For other settings use command:
itasca.command('model mechanical timestep safety-factor 0.9')
python_sdk_alternative.notes: Python SDK provides itasca.timestep() to get timestep, itasca.set_timestep() to set fixed timestep, and itasca.mech_age() to get accumulated mechanical time. For advanced settings, use itasca.command()

keywords.name: active
keywords.description: The mechanical process is on by default. Specify active off to suppress mechanical calculations.

keywords.name: dynamic
keywords.description: Specify the state of the dynamic process (requires `model configure dynamic`).

keywords.name: list
keywords.description: Display mechanical calculation-mode information.

keywords.name: time-total
keywords.description: f is the accumulated mechanical time for the model. This can be reset to any value, and will continue to be accumulated during cycling after that.

keywords.name: slave
keywords.description: Specifies that mechanical mode is to be considered a slave, and cycled to equilibrium after each master step has been taken.

keywords.name: substep
keywords.description: Specifies the maximum number of steps the mechanical process will take when solving with slave on .

keywords.name: timestep
keywords.description: Configure timestep behavior for the mechanical process. Sub-keywords: automatic (default; calculate stable timestep from current stiffnesses and masses), fix <f> (use fixed timestep <f>; calculation skipped), increment <f> (maximum per-cycle increase factor in automatic mode; default 1.05; timestep may decrease freely), maximum <f> (upper bound on timestep; unbounded by default), safety-factor <f> (multiplier on the calculated stable timestep in automatic mode; default 1.0), scale <f|on|off> (density-scaling mode for fast convergence to a static configuration; masses are scaled so the stable timestep equals f, or 1.0 if omitted; meaningful timescale is lost), update <i> (recompute timestep every <i> cycles; default 1). See `Mechanical Timestep Determination for Numerical Stability` for mass-scaling discussion.

examples.description: Suppress mechanical calculations (e.g., for thermal-only analysis)
Enable mechanical calculations (default)
Display mechanical calculation-mode information
Reset accumulated mechanical time to zero
Use automatic timestep calculation (default)
Set fixed timestep to 1e-6 for performance enhancement
Allow timestep to increase by 10% per cycle
Set maximum timestep to 1e-5
Use 90% of calculated stable timestep

examples.command:
model mechanical slave on
model mechanical substep 1000
examples.description: Set mechanical mode as slave with max 1000 substeps

examples.command:
model mechanical timestep automatic
model mechanical timestep safety-factor 0.8
model mechanical timestep maximum 1e-4
examples.description: Configure automatic timestep with safety factor and maximum limit
